title: ProjectSubstrateValidationSnapshot
description: Read-only canonical validation snapshot binding project substrate anchors to deterministic identity digests.

contract.description: Active canonical project-substrate contract used for validation.

validation_state.description: Validation posture state for canonical anchors and declared verified contract posture.

reason_codes.description: Stable reason-code set explaining invalid or missing posture.

runecontext_version.description: Declared RuneContext substrate version from canonical root config when parseable.

declared_assurance_tier.description: Declared assurance tier from canonical root config when parseable.

declared_source_type.description: Declared canonical source type from root config when parseable.

declared_source_path.description: Declared canonical source path from root config when parseable.

snapshot_digest.description: Canonical digest identity computed over normalized validation snapshot fields.

validated_snapshot_digest.description: Canonical validated snapshot identity digest when validation_state is valid.

project_context_identity_digest.description: Canonical project-context identity digest bound to validated substrate snapshot identity.

anchors.description: Canonical anchor and trust-surface checks for project substrate validation.

has_config_anchor.description: True when canonical repo-root runecontext.yaml exists.

has_source_anchor.description: True when canonical runecontext/ source root exists.

has_assurance_anchor.description: True when canonical runecontext/assurance/ path exists.

has_verified_posture.description: True when canonical root config declares verified assurance posture.

has_canonical_source.description: True when canonical root config declares source.path = runecontext.

has_private_truth_copy.description: True when non-canonical RuneCode-only mirror candidates are detected.

canonical_candidate_paths.description: Detected runecontext-related non-canonical candidate paths at repository root that can block explicit initialization.
